title: Tantalizing Crisp Algerian Beef Broth
description: A midnight crave low-carb beef broth featuring a cultured stock concentrate, a deglaze-fond and crisp zucchini garnish.
instructions: Place beef bones in a large pot and cover with 4 cups water; bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 45 minutes, skimming off foam.
Stir in 1 tbsp beef stock concentrate until dissolved; remove pot from heat.
In a separate skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium-high and sauté diced onion for 3 minutes until translucent.
Add minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, and smoked paprika; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in 2 tbsp tomato paste, coating the spices; cook for 2 minutes to deepen flavor.
Deglaze the skillet with a splash of water, scraping up browned bits, then pour the mixture into the simmering broth.
Return the pot to the stove, bring to a gentle simmer, and add sliced zucchini; cook for 4 minutes until tender yet crisp.
Stir in lemon juice, season with salt and pepper to taste, then remove from heat.
Ladle broth into bowls, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve hot.
